features.summary: JPMorgan Chase reported strong Q4 2024 results with net income of $14 billion and EPS of $4.81 on revenue of $43.7 billion, achieving a 21% ROTCE. For the full year, the firm delivered record revenue and net income with an ROTCE of 20%. The CET1 ratio increased to 15.7%, reflecting substantial excess capital generation. Management provided 2025 guidance of approximately $90 billion for NII ex-markets and $95 billion for expenses, with expectations for NII to trough mid-year before returning to growth.
features.management_outlook: Management expects 2025 NII ex-markets of approximately $90 billion with a potential trough in mid-year followed by growth in the second half. Card loan growth is expected to continue but decelerate from the 12% pace in 2024 as revolve normalization tailwinds diminish. Firm-wide deposits have stabilized with visible growth trends expected in the second half of 2025. The company plans to arrest further growth in excess capital through increased share repurchases while maintaining patience for better deployment opportunities.
features.risks: Key risks include potential stagflation scenarios combining higher rates with higher unemployment, which would drive credit losses across all segments. The firm acknowledges tension between current market optimism and underlying economic uncertainties. Regulatory uncertainty remains despite expectations for a more rational framework under the new administration. Credit normalization continues with wholesale credit potentially moving toward more historical loss rates after an extended period of exceptionally low charge-offs.
features.key_drivers: Primary revenue drivers include strong investment banking performance with 49% growth in fees and record markets revenue up 21% year-over-year. Asset & Wealth Management delivered record long-term net inflows of $234 billion with 18% growth in AUM. Card Services showed robust growth with 11% increase in outstandings driven by strong account acquisition. The firm's continued investment in technology, branch expansion, and talent acquisition supports long-term franchise growth across all business segments.
